data.title: Bitcoin OG Who Told People To Buy BTC At $1 Reveals How High XRP Price Will Go
data.description:
The crypto market is paying close attention after one of the most famous early Bitcoin voices shared a bold view on XRP. Davinci Jeremie, who gained notoriety for advising people to buy Bitcoin at just $1 back in 2013, has now issued a strong forecast for XRP, noting that the token’s chart displays a healthy structure and a bullish pattern. Davinci Jeremie Maps XRP Price Path To $4. 93 With Fibonacci Levels In his detailed breakdown, Jeremie focused on XRP’s recent movements and the structure forming on its chart
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/analysis/xrp/bitcoin-og-xrp-price-4-93/

Recent data from Crunchbase shows that over 5000 blockchain companies are currently located in the U. S. Yet this number is expected to grow, as the Trump administration seeks to position the U. S. as a global […]
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/why-global-blockchain-companies-are-moving-to-the-united-states/

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor's Legal Team Challenges Dismissal Over Mortgage Dispute
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the legal team representing Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook has argued that a mortgage dispute, which led U.S. President Donald Trump to seek her dismissal, may have originated from an "unintentional clerical error." This argument aims to counter allegations of fraudulent intent made by Trump and Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Mark Calabria. The lawyers assert that even if an error occurred, it was not deliberate deception, and no one suffered harm, which pertains to the legal standard of "materiality."

The legal team stated that Cook might have incorrectly marked the purpose of a property in a mortgage application long before joining the Federal Reserve Board, but there was no intent or substantial impact, which does not constitute "just cause" for removal. The lawyers also highlighted that neither Trump nor Calabria explicitly accused Cook of benefiting from the error or that it was intentional.

Previously, Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook filed a lawsuit against U.S. President Donald Trump, protesting his attempt to remove her from her position at the Federal Reserve.

data.title: SOL trades at the highest level since February as Solana DeFi grows
data.description:
Solana (SOL) expanded to its highest price range since February, rising briefly to over $215. The recent price rally coincided with DeFi app growth, both in terms of liquidity and development activity.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/sol-trades-at-the-highest-level-since-february-as-solana-defi-grows/

data.title: CFTC issues advisory to restore US access to foreign crypto exchanges
data.description:
The Commodity Futures Trading Commission’s (CFTC) Division of Market Oversight issued an advisory on Aug. 28 clarifying foreign board of trade (FBOT) registration rules for non-US exchanges seeking to provide Americans with direct market access. Acting Chair Caroline Pham positioned the guidance as a remedy for trading activity that departed during previous enforcement actions
source: https://cryptoslate.com/cftc-issues-advisory-to-restore-us-access-to-foreign-crypto-exchanges/

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway expands further into Japan with more stakes in Mitsubishi
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway has pushed its stake in Mitsubishi past the 10% threshold, Mitsubishi said Thursday in a formal statement. The holding, made through Berkshire’s insurance subsidiary National Indemnity Company, rose to 10. 23%, up from 9. 74% in March. This increase makes the U. S. conglomerate an even bigger shareholder in Japan’s biggest trading company. A Mitsubishi spokesperson […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/berkshire-hathaway-more-stakes-in-mitsubishi/
